items.definition_en: A sideboard is a piece of furniture used for storing dishes and serving food.
items.example_en: They placed the family photos on the sideboard in the dining room.

items.definition_en: An ottoman is a soft seat without a back or arms, often used as a footstool.
items.example_en: He put his feet up on the ottoman while reading.

items.definition_en: A cushion is a soft bag filled with material used for comfort.
items.example_en: She added another cushion to the sofa for extra comfort.

items.definition_en: A recliner is a comfortable chair that can lean back and extend the footrest.
items.example_en: He fell asleep in the recliner after a long day.

items.definition_en: A headboard is the upright part at the end of a bed where you rest your head.
items.example_en: She leaned back against the headboard while reading.

items.definition_en: A lounger is a long chair for reclining and relaxing.
items.example_en: She enjoyed her afternoon nap on the lounger.

items.definition_en: A china cabinet is a piece of furniture with glass doors used to display dishes.
items.example_en: The china cabinet in the dining room holds her grandmother's plates.

items.definition_en: Nesting tables are a set of small tables that fit under each other.
items.example_en: We pulled out the nesting tables when extra guests arrived.

items.definition_en: A chaise longue is a long upholstered chair for reclining.
items.example_en: She stretched out on the chaise longue with a book.

items.definition_en: A dressing table is a table with a mirror used for applying makeup.
items.example_en: Her dressing table was covered with brushes and perfumes.

items.definition_en: A display cabinet is a glass-fronted cabinet for showing objects.
items.example_en: Her collection of souvenirs is in the display cabinet.
